story: Ariana’s silver magic always twisted wishes: ask for silk, get a muddy boot; ask for stew, get fish. When the King summoned her to heal his fever-stricken son, she went anyway, certain he wanted her Gift, not her as a bride. In the dim chamber, the Prince lay pale and still while the old King begged. Real wizards had failed; Ariana sent the King out, meaning to run, but stayed and took the Prince’s cool hand, whispering to her unruly magic to listen. In his fever-dreams, the Prince drifted through fields and vowed that if life returned, he would owe no one his future—he would thank his healer and let her go free. Ariana wished him life. His breath hitched; his eyelids fluttered.
implausible_knowledge: In his fever-dreams, the Prince drifted through fields and vowed that if life returned, he would owe no one his future—he would thank his healer and let her go free.
question: Now that the Prince begins to wake, what will Ariana do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Stay by his bedside to hear his thanks and speak with him.
answer_without_knowledge: Slip out through the window before the court can stop her.
